zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabel Warcop於大約1300年出生在英國時，她的父親Thomas de Warcop I27歲，母親Margaret Helbeck23歲。她於大約1319年與William l'Engleys在英國結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於大約1365年在她的家鄉享年67歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Isabel Warcop于大约1300年出生在英国时，她的父亲Thomas de Warcop I27岁，母亲Margaret Helbeck23岁。她于大约1319年与William l'Engleys在英国结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于大约1365年在她的家乡享年67岁。

nameMeanings.name: Warcop
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Warcop (Westmorland). The placename derives from Old Norse vartha ‘cairn or beacon’ + Old English copp ‘top of a hill’.

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Isabel Warcop was born about 1300, in England, her father, Thomas de Warcop I, was 27 and her mother, Margaret Helbeck, was 23. She married William l'Engleys about 1319, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 3 daughters. She died about 1365, in her hometown, at the age of 67.

ko.lifeSummary: Isabel Warcop(이)가 대략 1300년 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas de Warcop I(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Helbeck(은)는 23세였다. 그녀는 대략 1319년 영국에서 William l'Engleys(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1365년 그녀의 고향에서 67세의 나이로 사망했다.
